---
title: "About WISSPAR"
---

## What is WISSPAR?

The **Worldwide Index of Serotype Specific Pneumococcal Antibody Responses
(WISSPAR)** is a centralized platform housing data on immunogenicity from
clinical trials of pneumococcal vaccines. Data are curated primarily from the
outcome tables reported on
[clinicaltrials.gov](https://clinicaltrials.gov){target="_blank"}, capturing
product details, manufacturers, dosing schedules, age groups, geographic
regions, assays (GMC and OPA), and serotype-specific antibody responses.

## Using the data responsibly

::: {.wisspar-caveat}
Immunogenicity data should be compared and interpreted with caution. Data on
immunogenicity **alone cannot** be used to infer differences in effectiveness
between vaccines. These data need to be combined with information on the
protective concentration of antibodies required to protect against each serotype
in different populations for meaningful comparisons. Caution should be used when
comparing data from trials conducted by different sponsors, which might use
different assays.
:::

## Collaboration & funding

WISSPAR is a collaboration between the **Yale School of Public Health** and the
**Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health**, with funding support from
the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation**.

### Contributors

- Stephanie Perniciaro
- Dominic Cooper-Wootton
- Maria Deloria Knoll
- Daniel M. Weinberger

## Contact

For inquiries, suggestions, or to request a custom private data-access portal for
a specific application, please get in touch with the WISSPAR team at
[daniel.weinberger@yale.edu](mailto:daniel.weinberger@yale.edu).

::: {layout-ncol=3 style="align-items:center; margin-top:2rem;"}
![Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation](assets/images/gates-foundation-logo.png){fig-alt="Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation logo"}

![Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health](assets/images/jhs.PNG){fig-alt="Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health logo"}

![Yale School of Public Health](assets/images/ysph-removebg-preview.png){fig-alt="Yale School of Public Health logo"}
:::
